---
title: "ML & Autonomous Driving Notes"
description: "Deep-dive technical reports on machine learning and autonomous-driving systems — with executed notebooks, math, and runnable code."
toc: false
---

Working notes and survey reports on the systems behind modern autonomous driving and
machine learning: planning benchmarks, edge inference, and model compression. Each report
is written to be *mechanistic* — it explains why something works, not just that it does —
and most ship with an executed notebook or a runnable script.

The four below are the flagship pieces. The [full archive of ~30 reports and notebooks
lives on GitHub](https://github.com/morimori0456/ML_report).

## Featured

::: {.grid}

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-6}
### [How NAVSIM Computes PDMS](autonomous_driving/driving_benchmarks/navsim_pdms_mechanism.md)
Why a *non-reactive, not-closed-loop* benchmark can still score collisions and
time-to-collision. Untangles the two meanings of "closed-loop," walks the LQR + bicycle
ego rollout, the gated score formula, and why a 4-second non-reactive window is a faithful
proxy — with a from-scratch PDMS calculator.

`autonomous driving` · `planning eval` · `NAVSIM / nuPlan`
:::

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-6}
### [TensorRT Version Compatibility](edge_deployment/tensorrt_versioning.md)
Surviving TensorRT's dependency chain (driver → CUDA → cuDNN → TensorRT → engine). Why a
`.plan` built yesterday won't load today, how JetPack pins the whole stack, the version-
and hardware-compatible escape hatches, and a troubleshooting decision tree.

`edge deployment` · `TensorRT` · `Jetson`
:::

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-6}
### [TensorRT INT8 Quantization on Jetson](edge_deployment/tensorrt_quantization_jetson.md)
A device-side recipe to quantize an AD-perception model to INT8: the affine mapping, what
the entropy calibrator actually computes, PTQ vs QAT, the build/benchmark commands, and
the Jetson Thor FP8/FP4 silent-fallback trap.

`edge deployment` · `quantization` · `INT8 / FP8`
:::

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-6}
### [Knowledge Distillation — Complete Guide](distillation/knowledge_distillation.md)
The response / feature / relation families, from logit KD through FitNets, Attention
Transfer, FSP, NST, PKT, RKD, CRD, OFD and ReviewKD — with the adapters, transforms, and
loss-weighting that make them work in practice.

`model compression` · `distillation`
:::

:::

---

*Comments are open at the bottom of each report. The source for every page is on
[GitHub](https://github.com/morimori0456/ML_report) — corrections and discussion welcome.*
